records.ety: [OE. middel, AS. middel; akin to D. middel, OHG. muttil, G. mittel. √271. See Mid, a.]

defs: Equally distant from the extreme either of a number of things or of one thing; mean; medial; as, the middle house in a row; a middle rank or station in life; flowers of middle summer; men of middle age.

quotes: Will, seeking good, finds many middle ends.
The middle-class electorate of Great Britain.
notes: ☞ Middle is sometimes used in the formation of self-explaining compounds; as, middle-sized, middle-witted.

defs: The point or part equally distant from the extremities or exterior limits, as of a line, a surface, or a solid; an intervening point or part in space, time, or order of series; the midst; central portion
quotes: In this, as in most questions of state, there is a middle.
